message.abstract:
<jats:title>Abstract</jats:title>
                <jats:p>
                  We describe an elemental logistic model for the propagation of CoV-2 in a community and illustrate the sensitivity of the model to key parameters such as
                  <jats:bold>R</jats:bold>
                  <jats:sub>
                    <jats:bold>0</jats:bold>
                  </jats:sub>
                  , the initial rate of infections per infected person, and
                  <jats:bold>A</jats:bold>
                  <jats:sub>
                    <jats:bold>0</jats:bold>
                  </jats:sub>
                  , the fraction of infected people developing neutralizing antibodies. We demonstrate the importance of the
                  <jats:bold>duration</jats:bold>
                  of immunity in the population, the development of waves of new cases of infection, and the effect of premature opening of local economies.
                </jats:p>
title: A logistic model of CoV-2 propagation

reference.unstructured: Centers for Disease Control and Prevention, https://www.cdc.gov, Data and Statistics (July, 2020)

Altmann, D , Imperial College, London, UK, on CNBC “Squaw Box Europe”, 1 June, 2020. See footnote *
St. Louis Post-Dispatch , “Health director says spike in Missouri coronavirus deaths due to reporting delays”: 21 May, 2020)
